subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content: 72 double-sided word cards ("This Side" and "That Side", over 800 words total), 6 erasable sketch books, 6 dry-erase markers, 3 clean-up cloths, and 1 die.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content: Place cards, cloths, and die in the center. As a group, choose to use either THIS SIDE or THAT SIDE for all cards this game. Each player takes 1 sketch book, 1 marker, and 1 card. Every player writes their name on the front of their sketch book.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
Each player rolls the die (or one die result is used in turn) and matches the number to the corresponding word on their own card. Secretly write that word in your sketch book. If the matched space is blank, invent any word. If the matched item is an underlined category, invent a word in that category.

Player-count adjustment before the first pass:
- 4 or 6 players: each player starts by drawing their own secret word on SKETCH IT page 1.
- 5 players: each player opens to SKETCH IT page 1 and passes left first; the receiving player reads the secret word and draws it. This ensures the chain ends on a guess.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
The game is played over 3 rounds. Each round follows this sequence:
1. Get a Word: Determine each player's secret word from die result and card entry, and record it secretly.
2. First Sketch: On SKETCH IT page 1, draw the word (or in 5-player games, draw the passed word as described in setup).
3. Hide and Pass: Turn to page 2 to hide the drawing and pass the open book to the player on your left.
4. Guess: Receiver flips back to view prior sketch, then writes a guess on GUESS IT page 2.
5. Pass Again: Flip forward and pass left simultaneously.
6. Alternate Sketch/Guess: Continue alternating sketch pages and guess pages each pass until books return to original owners. The sequence should end with a guess.
7. Big Reveal: Owners flip through their books in order, sharing each sketch and guess chain aloud.
8. Score Round: Apply either Friendly or Competitive scoring. After 3 rounds, highest total wins.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
On each pass, a player performs exactly one content action plus one pass action:

A) Sketch action
- Draw the current word/guess prompt on the open SKETCH IT page.
- Sketches must contain no letters and no numbers.
- Complete within roughly one minute to maintain game pace.

B) Guess action
- Flip back to view only the immediately previous sketch.
- Write your best guess on the open GUESS IT page.
- You must write a guess even if uncertain or silly.

C) Pass action
- After sketching or guessing, flip pages so prior content is hidden and pass the book left.
- All players pass simultaneously to keep chains synchronized.

Global restrictions
- No blank pages are allowed.
- No letters or numbers in any sketch.
- Books are always passed open to the page the next player must complete.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
Chain transformation system:
- Each book becomes a telephone-style chain: word -> sketch -> guess -> sketch -> guess, and so on. Accuracy and humor emerge from how information mutates.

5-player parity rule:
- The special first-pass handling in 5-player games exists only to ensure the final page is a guess, matching the intended reveal/scoring flow.

Variants from rulebook:
- Change seats between rounds or reverse passing direction.
- Require sketching with the less-dominant hand.
- Create custom word cards.
- For mixed ages, let youngest players pick any word freely.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
End trigger:
- Play exactly 3 rounds, then total points. Highest score wins.

Scoring mode 1: Friendly Scoring
- +1 point to the player with your favorite sketch (your choice).
- +1 point to the player with your favorite guess (your choice).
- +1 point to yourself if the last guess in your own book matches your original secret word.

Scoring mode 2: Competitive Scoring
- Guesser earns +1 point when their guess matches either the original secret word or the previous guess in chain.
- Sketcher earns +1 point when their sketch helps the next guesser match correctly.
- +1 point to yourself if the final guess in your own book matches your original secret word.

All point sources to count:
- Favorite sketch votes (friendly mode only).
- Favorite guess votes (friendly mode only).
- Correct guess links (competitive mode only).
- Helpful sketch links (competitive mode only).
- End-of-chain self-match bonus (both modes).

Tie-breaker:
- The provided rule text states only "most points wins" and does not define a formal tie-break. If tied, treat as shared win or play an agreed extra round.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
1) Favor clarity over artistry in your first sketches. Large, iconic shapes communicate better than detailed drawings.
2) If you get a blank/category prompt, pick a concrete noun (easy to draw and guess) rather than an abstract concept.
3) During guesses, commit to the most literal interpretation of visible clues; over-clever guesses often derail the chain.
4) Keep pace and pass cleanly. Fast, synchronized passing prevents accidental extra information leakage and confusion.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
1) Drawing tiny details instead of recognizable silhouettes. Result: guessers miss the core idea.
2) Breaking the no letters/numbers rule, which gives illegal clues and can invalidate the spirit of the round.
3) Leaving pages blank or skipping uncertain guesses. The chain must stay unbroken for scoring and reveal flow.
4) Passing out of sync. If players pass at different times, page order mistakes happen and books get mismatched.
5) Choosing overly niche words when blanks/categories allow custom picks, making fair guessing nearly impossible for the table.

metadata.notes: Built directly from provided 6 Player Family Pack rule text; includes both scoring modes and listed variants.
